title: attributes — authorization policies
description: Three policies — one per aggregate. Attributes are tenant-configurable but require elevated permissions to modify — a bad AttributeSet cascades to every host using it. Values validated on the host's own save path; no direct value-mutation policies here.

abilities.scope: admin + branch_manager + hr — read the tenant's attribute catalogue
admin + owner. Small tier uses seeded defaults only; Medium+ can define custom via `attributes.custom_definitions` entitlement
admin + owner. Type + validation changes flagged as breaking — trigger set versioning cascade
admin + owner. Sets referencing this definition remain valid (historic render); new sets refuse the definition
admin + owner. Refused when referenced by any AttributeSet (existing or superseded)
admin + owner. Draft state — activation is a separate policy
admin + owner. Definition_order + labels — no version bump
admin + owner. Explicit breaking-change acknowledgement. Creates new version, supersedes old
admin + owner. Refuses when another active set exists at same (entity_type, discriminator_value)
admin + owner. Falls back to next-lower version if any
admin + owner. Refused when any host record snapshots this set version
admin + owner. Groups belong to a set — parent set must be draft or version-eligible
admin + owner. Labels + icon + collapsible — no version bump. sort_order changes fire AttributeGroupReordered
admin + owner. Cascade deletes from parent set
